title: arabis serpillifolia subsp. serpillifolia
intro: Arabis serpillifolia subsp. serpillifolia, also known as Maire's rock-cress or maire's cress, is a plant species within the Brassicaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, and has been used to treat coughs, colds, and sore throats. Arabis serpillifolia subsp. serpillifolia can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and its leaves and stems can be used fresh or dried for herbal preparations.
